demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Parent] Please help — he just suddenly lost all muscle tone and smashed his face on the table, and now he won't stop having these stiffening seizures. He's not properly waking up between them.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He collapsed about half an hour ago, cut his face open on the way down, and has had one stiffening fit after another since — we can't get it to stop.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 30 minutes ago he suddenly went completely floppy and dropped, hitting his face on the table
symptom_narrative.duration: ongoing for about half an hour — one fit after another
symptom_narrative.character: first he went limp and fell, then he keeps going rigid and stiff all over for a couple of minutes at a time
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst cluster we've seen — he normally comes round, but this time he hasn't woken up properly between them
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his whole body goes stiff; there's a deep cut above his left eyebrow from the fall
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: repeated stiffening episodes, roughly 2 to 3 minutes each, back to back
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing we've tried has stopped it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps happening on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he's bleeding from the cut on his eyebrow and now he's very drowsy and floppy and not really looking at us
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has a lot of seizures normally with his epilepsy, including sudden drop attacks, but never a run like this without waking up
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he can't respond or communicate at the moment; he's usually got significant developmental delay and needs full care

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a severe childhood epilepsy — he was diagnosed with Lennox-Gastaut Syndrome and has many different kinds of seizures, including drop attacks
past_medical_history.how_managed: seen by a paediatric neurologist and on several daily seizure medicines

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: developmental delay and intellectual disability with gradual regression
past_medical_history.how_managed: supported at home and through his specialist team

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: six-year-old, lives with and is fully cared for by both parents, who are present and very distressed; the parents mention they are exhausted and struggling with his care

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known significant developmental delay and intellectual disability with progressive regression; at baseline requires full care and does not communicate verbally

objective_data_offered.value: a deep cut roughly 3 cm long above his left eyebrow that is still oozing blood
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer/video description only — depth and need for stitches cannot be assessed remotely

objective_data_offered.value: he keeps going stiff and rigid for two to three minutes at a time and has not fully woken up in between the episodes
he suddenly went completely floppy and fell face-first onto the table before the stiffening started
right now he is very drowsy and floppy, not tracking us with his eyes, and only stirs a little when touched
he missed his morning dose of one of his seizure medicines (clobazam) today
